title: DSDS Component Definitions
description: Definitions for the composite component document. A component document captures the identity and metadata of a reusable UI element. All structured docs — anatomy, API, variants, states, design specs, best practices, accessibility specs, use cases, and examples — live in the unified `guidelines` array as typed guideline objects.

component.description: A single reusable UI component. Identity (name, description, status, category) lives at the top level. All structured docs live in the `guidelines` array — each entry is a typed guideline object (anatomy, api, variant, state, design-specifications, best-practice, accessibility, etc.). This unified model replaces separate top-level properties for each concern.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a component.

name.description: Machine-readable identifier (e.g., 'button', 'dialog', 'form-field', 'data-table'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

extends.description: Declares that this component inherits from a base component in a parent design system. The base provides the foundational anatomy, API, variants, states, and guidelines. This component layers on top — adding variants, props, guidelines, or specialized behavior while inheriting everything else.

aliases.description: Alternative names for this component across teams, tools, or legacy systems (e.g., ['btn', 'action-button', 'CTA']). Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing.

displayName.description: Human-readable name for display in docs (e.g., 'Button', 'Dialog', 'Form Field', 'Data Table').

summary.description: A one-line, plain-text summary for compact contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards. MUST NOT contain markup.

thumbnail.description: A visual for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards.

description.description: A description of the component — what it is, what it does, and its role in the design system. CommonMark supported.

preview.description: A visual or interactive preview of the component — a hero image, video walkthrough, code snippet, or link to a live demo. Accepts any presentation type.

since.description: The design system version in which this component was introduced (e.g., '1.0.0', '2.3.0').

tags.description: Freeform keywords for categorization, search, and cross-referencing (e.g., 'action', 'form', 'interactive', 'navigation', 'feedback'). Tags are not constrained to a vocabulary.

category.description: Component classification within the design system's taxonomy (e.g., 'action', 'navigation', 'feedback', 'data-display', 'layout', 'form', 'overlay'). Used for organizing components into sections in documentation sites and component libraries.

guidelines.description: All structured docs for this component as an ordered array of typed guideline objects. Accepts component-specific types (anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) and general types (best-practices, purpose, accessibility, examples). Each guideline has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Express internal artifact relationships through the `links` array rather than as guidelines.

links.description: External resource links, internal artifact references, and entity relationships — source code, design files, Storybook stories, docs pages, npm packages, related components, parent/child relationships, and alternatives. Each link points to an external URL, an internal DSDS artifact by name, or both.
